name: Mun
description: A programming language empowering creation through iteration, focused on hot reloading for game development

implementation.comment: Runtime is pure Rust, but compiler requires LLVM 14 (C++)

language-family.comment: Syntax heavily inspired by Rust with similar constructs

latest-version.comment: Released December 12, 2022. v0.6.0 in progress (85% complete)

typing.comment: Statically typed with type inference. Types resolved at compile time.

paradigm.comment: Primarily imperative. No closures or first-class functions yet.

first-class-functions.comment: Functions cannot be passed as values or stored in variables

pattern-matching.comment: No pattern matching constructs. Only if/else and loops.

error-handling.comment: No Result type or exceptions in scripts. Runtime errors abort execution.

iterators-generators.comment: No iterator protocol or generators. Arrays added in v0.4 but no iteration constructs.

module-system.comment: Multi-file projects supported with 'use' keyword for imports

standard-library.comment: No standard library. Only primitive types and basic operators.

execution-strategy.comment: Ahead-of-time compiled to native machine code via LLVM

compilation-model.comment: Must be compiled before execution. No runtime eval.

hot-reloading.comment: First-class hot reloading - the core feature. Detects recompiled code and reloads automatically.

repl-support.comment: AOT-compiled language with no REPL support

source-maps.comment: Runtime errors logged with source location information

startup-time.comment: Loads precompiled native libraries. No JIT warmup.

execution-speed.comment: LLVM-compiled to native machine code for maximum performance

memory-overhead.comment: Lightweight runtime for loading and hot-reloading compiled modules

garbage-collection.comment: Garbage-collected heap allocation for struct types

pure-rust.comment: Requires LLVM 14 for compilation. Runtime is Rust but compiler needs C++ LLVM.

api-safety.comment: Runtime uses some unsafe internally for FFI with compiled modules

idiomatic-api.comment: Functional API but requires manual setup. Hot reloading needs explicit update() calls.

async-compatible.comment: Runtime runs synchronously. Update loop must be driven by host.

function-registration.comment: Extern functions declared in Mun and linked at runtime

type-mapping.comment: Explicit marshaling between Rust and Mun types via ABI layer

no-std-compatible.comment: Depends on std for file system operations and dynamic library loading

wasm-compatible.comment: WebAssembly modules listed as a target use case

blank-slate-mode.comment: No standard library - scripts only see explicitly provided extern functions

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory. All interaction via defined ABI.

cpu-limits.comment: No instruction count or time limits - native code execution

syscall-restrictions.comment: No built-in file/network I/O. All capabilities via extern functions.

default-deny.comment: Scripts can only access explicitly linked extern functions

call-rust-from-script.comment: Extern functions allow calling Rust from Mun scripts

call-script-from-rust.comment: Runtime.invoke() calls Mun functions from Rust

share-data.comment: Data marshaled between Rust and Mun via ABI. Structs support hot reloading.

custom-types.comment: Hot reloadable structs supported since v0.2.0

method-syntax.comment: Method calls and associated functions added in recent development

last-release.comment: v0.4.0 released Dec 12, 2022. Active development continues toward v0.6.0

editor-support.comment: VS Code and Vim/Neovim extensions available. LSP support included.

example-projects.comment: Primary focus is game development with hot reloading. Born from Abbey Games' needs.

maintenance-status.comment: Active commits through May 2025. v0.6.0 milestone at 85% complete.

documentation-quality.comment: Official book with getting-started guide, language reference, and examples

maturity.comment: Explicitly stated 'in the early stages of development and nowhere near production-ready'

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed specifically for game development hot reloading. Console/mobile/XR targets.
